title: aira praecox var. caryophyllea
intro: Aira praecox var. caryophyllea, also known as the wood meadow-grass, is a variety of grass native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Aira praecox var. caryophyllea include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its potential to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
